examples: create a temporary global region named "temporary_global_region" in the player's world
create region "cuboid_region" in player's world between the location (0, 60, 0) and the location (10, 70, 10)
create a polygonal region named "polygonal_region" with a minimum height of 10 and a maximum height of 20 with points {points::*}
patterns: create [a] [temporary] polygonal [worldguard] region [named] %string% [in %world%] [with [a] min[imum] height of %integer% and [a] max[imum] height of %integer%] with [the] points %locations%
EffCreateRegion.name: Create Region
EffCreateRegion.description:
An effect to create a WorldGuard region.
A temporary region is a region that will not be saved, meaning it is lost when the server restarts.
A global region is a region that has no boundaries, meaning it covers the entire world.
A cuboid region is the traditional WorldGuard region. It has two points to determine the boundaries.
A polygonal region comprises many points. These points are used to draw a two-dimensional shape. Then, with the provided heights, the shape is extended vertically to form the region. At least three points must be provided to create a polygonal region.
Note that if you do not specify the world for a region, you must be sure that the locations provided all have the same world.
Note that Region IDs are only valid if they contain letters, numbers, underscores, commas, single quotation marks, dashes, pluses, and forward slashes.
Note that if you attempt to create a region in a world where a region with the same ID already exists, that region will be replaced.
Note that if you do not specify the minimum and maximum heights for a polygonal region, those values will be calculated from the points.

examples: region "region" in world("world"
TypeWorldguardRegion.name: Region

examples:
on region enter:
	player is the owner of the region
	message "Welcome back to %region%"
	message "%player's name% just entered %region%" to the members of the region
CondIsRegionMemberOwner.name: Is Region Member/Owner
CondIsRegionMemberOwner.description: A condition to test whether a player/group is a member/owner of a region.

examples:
command /setblock <material>:
	description: Sets your targeted block to a different type.
	trigger:
		if the player cannot build at the targeted block:
			message "<red>You do not have permission to modify your targeted block!"
		else:
			set the targeted block to the material-argument
CondCanBuildInRegions.name: Can Build In Regions
CondCanBuildInRegions.description: A condition to test whether a player can build in a region or at a specific location.

CondIsRegionTemporary.name: Is Region Temporary
CondIsRegionTemporary.description:
A condition to test whether a region is temporary.
Temporary regions are those that are removed when the server restarts.

CondIsRegionType.name: Is Region Type
CondIsRegionType.description: A condition to test what type/shape a region is.

examples:
command /printchildren <text>:
	trigger:
		set {_parent} to the region text-argument
		message "Children of %{_parent}%:"
		loop all regions:
			if {_parent} is a parent region of loop-region:
				message "- %loop-region%"
patterns: %worldguardregions% (isn't|is not|aren't|are not) a parent region[s] of %worldguardregions%
CondIsParentRegion.name: Is Parent Region
CondIsParentRegion.description: A condition to test whether a region is a parent region of another.

examples: create a temporary global region named "temporary_global_region" in the player's world
create region "cuboid_region" in player's world between the location (0, 60, 0) and the location (10, 70, 10)
create a polygonal region named "polygonal_region" with a minimum height of 10 and a maximum height of 20 with points {points::*}
patterns: create [a] [temporary] polygonal [worldguard] region [named] %string% [in %world%] [with [a] min[imum] height of %integer% and [a] max[imum] height of %integer%] with [the] points %locations%

A temporary region is a region that will not be saved, meaning it is lost when the server restarts.
A global region is a region that has no boundaries, meaning it covers the entire world.
A cuboid region is the traditional WorldGuard region. It has two points to determine the boundaries.
A polygonal region comprises many points. These points are used to draw a two-dimensional shape. Then, with the provided heights, the shape is extended vertically to form the region. At least three points must be provided to create a polygonal region.
Note that if you do not specify the world for a region, you must be sure that the locations provided all have the same world.
Note that Region IDs are only valid if they contain letters, numbers, underscores, commas, single quotation marks, dashes, pluses, and forward slashes.
Note that if you attempt to create a region in a world where a region with the same ID already exists, that region will be replaced.
Note that if you do not specify the minimum and maximum heights for a polygonal region, those values will be calculated from the points.

examples:
command /createregion <text>:
	if the text-argument is not a valid region id:
		message "<red>'%text-argument%' is not a valid region ID")
	# here is where the rest of the command would go :)
CondIsValidRegionId.name: Is Valid Region ID
CondIsValidRegionId.description:
A condition to test whether a string is a valid region ID.
Valid region IDs only contain letters, numbers, underscores, commas, single quotation marks, dashes, pluses, or forward slashes.

CondIsValidRegionId.name: Is Valid Region ID
CondIsValidRegionId.description:
A condition to test whether a string is a valid region ID.
Valid region IDs only contain letters, numbers, underscores, commas, single quotation marks, dashes, pluses, or forward slashes.

examples:
command /highlight-points <text>:
	trigger:
		set the blocks at the points of the region text-argument to red wool
ExprRegionPoints.name: Region Points
ExprRegionPoints.description:
An expression to obtain the points of a region.
Note that when obtaining the points of a region, the region itself is projected onto an X-Z plane. Thus, the points do not have a y-component. However, for simplicity, the y-component of the minimum point is used.

examples:
command /fillregion <text> <material>:
	trigger:
		set {_region} to the region text-argument in the player's world
		set the blocks of {_region} to material-argument
ExprBlocksInRegion.name: Blocks of Region
ExprBlocksInRegion.description: An expression that returns all of the blocks in the given regions.

ExprMoveType.name: WorldGuard Move Type
ExprMoveType.description: The movement type in a 'region enter/leave' event. This represents how the player ended up in/out of a region.

ExprRegionPriority.name: Region Priority
ExprRegionPriority.description:
An expression to obtain and change the priority of a region.
When regions overlap, the priority is used to determine properties such as membership, ability to build, and flag values.

examples:
on right click:
	the clicked block is tagged with minecraft tag "all_signs"
	line 1 of the clicked block is "[Region Info]"
	set {_regions::*} to the regions at the clicked block
	if {_regions::*} is not set:
		message "No regions exist at this sign."
	else:
		message "You are in: %{_regions::*}%."
ExprRegionsAt.name: Regions At
ExprRegionsAt.description:
An expression to obtain the regions at a specific location.
Note that the regions will be returned in ascending order by priority.

ExprRegionMembersOwners.name: Region Members/Owners
ExprRegionMembersOwners.description:
An expression to obtain the members/owners of the given regions.
The members/owners of a region are made up of players and groups (strings).
By default, this expression returns both. However, keyword specifiers for each type (player/group) are available.

examples: the region "region" in world("world"
all of the regions in the player's world
ExprRegions.name: Regions
ExprRegions.description:
An expression to obtain all regions of a specific world or the region with a specific name in a world.
Please note that region names (IDs) are case insensitive.

examples:
command /setparent <text> <text>:
	trigger:
		set the parent of the region text-argument-1 to the region text-argument-2
if any of all of the parent regions of {_region} are global
ExprRegionParent.name: Region Parent
ExprRegionParent.description:
An expression to obtain and change the direct parent of a region.
It is also possible to obtain all parents of a region (e.g., including a parent region's parent region).
When a region has a parent, it inherits the parents members, owners, and flags (that aren't defined on the child).

examples: message flag "greeting" of player's region
ExprRegionFlag.name: Region Flag
ExprRegionFlag.description:
An expression for working with the flag of a region.
The region group flag of a flag supports controlling which groups of a region (i.e., members, owners, etc.) a flag applies to.
NOTE: Skript may not support all flags, specifically those representing values Skript cannot understand.

examples:
on region enter:
	send "You have entered %region%"
ExprRegion.name: Region
ExprRegion.description: The region in any region related event.

examples:
on region enter:
	send "You entered %region%"
EvtRegionEnterLeave.name: On Region Enter/Leave
EvtRegionEnterLeave.description: Called when a player enters or leaves a region.
